metadata.title: Archivos base para la modelación hidráulica en HEC-RAS del río Molino, Popayán, Cauca
metadata.description:
Este conjunto de archivos corresponde a los insumos base empleados para el desarrollo de un ejercicio académico de modelación hidráulica 1D en HEC-RAS, aplicado a un tramo urbano del río Molino en la ciudad de Popayán, Cauca.

Los archivos se organizan en carpetas temáticas que permiten estructurar el proceso de construcción del modelo hidráulico. La carpeta 01_shp contiene el shapefile de coberturas utilizado como insumo para la definición espacial de condiciones del modelo. La carpeta 02_raster incluye la ortofotografía y el modelo digital de elevación empleados para la preparación del terreno y la interpretación del tramo de estudio. La carpeta 03_prj contiene el archivo de proyección asociado al sistema de coordenadas MAGNA Colombia Oeste. La carpeta 04_caudales incluye la serie de tiempo de caudales máximos utilizada como referencia hidrológica para el ejercicio. Finalmente, la carpeta 05_hec se deja vacía como espacio sugerido para almacenar los archivos generados durante el proceso de modelación en HEC-RAS.

Este material se comparte con fines estrictamente académicos y busca apoyar el aprendizaje práctico de la modelación hidráulica 1D, incluyendo la preparación del terreno, incorporación de ortofotografía, definición de rugosidades, generación de secciones transversales, configuración de condiciones de flujo permanente y análisis de resultados hidráulicos.

Los archivos pueden ser utilizados como material complementario del video tutorial asociado, orientado a la construcción de un modelo hidráulico 1D en HEC-RAS para la evaluación de profundidades, velocidades de flujo y áreas inundables en un entorno urbano.

El modelo hidráulico asociado al ejercicio se desarrolló mediante HEC-RAS, software del Hydrologic Engineering Center del U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, empleado para análisis hidráulico en una y dos dimensiones, incluyendo cálculos de flujo permanente, flujo no permanente, transporte de sedimentos y calidad del agua.
references: U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Hydrologic Engineering Center. (2026). HEC-RAS River Analysis System, User's Manual. Hydrologic Engineering Center
U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Hydrologic Engineering Center. (2026). HEC-RAS River Analysis System [Computer software]. Hydrologic Engineering Center

data.title: Archivos base para la modelación hidráulica en HEC-RAS del río Molino, Popayán, Cauca

metadata.title: Dahlibruchus castroi Romero 2025, new species
metadata.description:
Dahlibruchus castroi Romero, new species

Type series. HoLotyPe MaLe, aLLotyPe feMaLe and 38 PaRatyPes: México, DistRito FedeRaL, kM 42 caRReteRa fedeRaL México-CueRnavaca, 30/IX/2000, 2475 M, J. RoMeRo N., ReaRed seed DAHLiA Rudis (CEAM); 18 PaRatyPes saMe data, excePt 29/IX/2000 (6 USNM, 6 CNIN, 6 FSCA); 10 PaRatyPes saMe data excePt 12/IX/2002; 17 PaRatyPes: DistRito FedeRaL, kM 46 autoPista México-CueRnavaca, 16/IX/2001, 2475 M, J. RoMeRo N., ReaRed seed DAHLiA Rudis SoRensen (CEAM); one PaRatyPe: México, DuRango, Las Adjuntas, PuebLo Nuevo, 11/IX/2021, 2720 M, CastRo C.A. & L. MaRtínez R., on DAHLiA sHeRffii (CEAM); one PaRatyPe: México, Estado de México, Santa CataRina deL Monte, 12 kM ESE Texcoco, 20/ VI /2000, 2800 M, Westcott R.L., beating QueRcus sP. (CEAM).

DeScription. Male (Fig. 1a–b). Length (PRonotuM–eLytRa): 1.65–2.52 MM; width: 0.9–1.08 MM; MaxiMuM thoRacic dePth 0.6–0.99 MM. Color: Body bLack, excePt antennae with fiRst fouR segMents yeLLowish to PaRtiaLLy daRk. Vestiture: Body with white Pubescence, but denseR on ePiMeRon, MetePisteRnuM, and LateRaL PoRtion of hind coxa. Head: ShoRt and bRoad, denseLy MicRoPunctate, excePt LabRuM and cLyPeus iMPunctate; fRons without Median caRina, distance between eyes 0.16–0.2× as wide as eye width, eye cLeft 0.50–0.70× its Length by ocuLaR sinus, PosteRioR MaRgin of eye PRotRuding fRoM adjacent suRfaces, PostocuLaR Lobe Rounded and setose; distance fRoM base of antennae to aPex of LabRuM 0.42–0.51× as Long as distance fRoM uPPeR LiMits of eyes to aPex of LabRuM; antennoMeRes I–IV fiLifoRM, V –X eccentRic, XI subacute aPicaLLy; antenna extending to base of eLytRon oR sLightLy beyond. Prothorax: SubconicaL, sides sLightLy aRcuate, with faint LateRaL caRina extending 0.33–0.5 distance fRoM base to coxaL cavity, denseLy foveoLate, Median basaL Lobe deLiMited by sLight eMaRginations; PRosteRnaL PRocess tRianguLaR, acute, sePaRating PRocoxae foR about 0.6–0.8 theiR Length. MeSo- and metathorax: ScuteLLuM squaRe, bidentate aPicaLLy; eLytRon 2.5–2.8× as Long as bRoad; eLytRa with stRiaL PunctuRes inconsPicuous, stRiae 3–5 abbReviated basaLLy by sMaLL tubeRcLe, huMeRi sLightLy Raised; MesosteRnuM with sMaLL PosteRioR suLcus, it without sMaLL Raised LateRaL MaRgins basaLLy. Legs: FiRst PRotaRsoMeRe about as Long as second, fiRst MesotaRsoMeRe 1.66–1.92× as Long as second, fiRst MetataRsoMeRe 2.0–2.77× as Long as second; foRetibia aRMed with a sPine neaR MiddLe of ventRaL MaRgin; Metacoxa foveoLate onLy in basaL PoRtion; hind feMuR constRicted basaLLy and aPicaLLy, exPanded MediaLLy to about width of coxa; without exteRnaL and inteRnaL caRina ventRaLLy; inteRnaL ventRaL MaRgin without a sPine; hind tibia stRaight, enLaRged, with onLy ventRaL caRina; tibiaL coRona with about fouR sMaLL sPines, MucRo with the saMe size as the otheR tibiaL coRona sPines. Abdomen: FiRst visibLe abdoMinaL steRnite LongeR than ReMaining steRnites; steRnites 2–4 siMiLaR in size; fifth sLightLy eMaRginate MediaLLy; PygidiuM fineLy foveoLate, with the basaL PoRtion gLobose; Last steRnite sLightLy eMaRginate. Genitalia: Median Lobe ModeRate in Length; in ventRaL view, ventRaL vaLve tRianguLaRLy Rounded, aRcuate in LateRaL view; inteRnaL sac veRy Long and foLded, aRMatuRe of inteRnaL sac with a sMaLL Mass of fine sPicuLes aPicaLLy and a LaRge Mass coMPosed of fine sPicuLes extending fRoM neaR MiddLe to neaR base (Fig. 3a). LateRaL Lobes eLongate, cLeft to about 0.78 theiR Length (Fig. 3b), aPicaL PoRtion sLightLy exPanded and divided by a sMaLL thin Ridge, the aPicaL PoRtion naRRoweR and with about 12 aPicaL Long setae, the basaL PoRtion wideR and with about thRee setae LateRaLLy. Female. SiMiLaR to MaLe, excePt the 5th abdoMinaL steRnite not eMaRginate, foRetibia without sPine on ventRaL MaRgin. Length (PRonotuM–eLytRa) 1.77–2.27 MM; width 1.0–1.25 MM; MaxiMuM thoRacic dePth 0.60–1.2 MM (Fig. 2a, b).

Host plants. DAHLiA Rudis and D. sHeRffii SoRensen.

Distribution. Mexico (DuRango, Estado de México, DistRito FedeRaL).

Etymology. The sPecific ePithet honoRs a gReat botanist and veRy good coLLectoR that heLPed Me coLLect insects and identify DAHLiA sPecies, DR. ARtuRo CastRo CastRo.

Diagnosis. This sPecies can be sePaRated fRoM the otheRs by the bLack coLoR of the body; aLso, theRe aRe sPecific chaRacteRs in the LateRaL Lobes of MaLe genitaLia that aRe unique foR this sPecies, such as shaPe of aPicaL PoRtion and the cLeft.

Discussion. DAHLiBRucHus cAstRoi is siMiLaR to D. conRAdti because both PResent the foRetibia of MaLe aRMed with a sPine on inteRnaL ventRaL MaRgin (Fig. 1b); howeveR, they can be sePaRated because aLL Legs in D. conRAdti aRe yeLLowish, the fRons has a Median caRina, and the basaL PoRtion of the feMaLe PygidiuM is not gLobose; whiLe in D. cAstRoi the Legs aRe bLack, the fRons is without a Median caRina, and the feMaLe PygidiuM has the basaL PoRtion gLobose (Fig. 2a). ALso, theRe aRe diffeRences in the MaLe genitaLia between the two sPecies.
